labels.text: T1N — 100/5 A, PX, Neutral CT for REF
Pickup 100% In = 100 A primary, SI curve, TMS 0.2
Pickup 5% In = 5% of CT secondary, high-impedance scheme with 470 ohm stabilising resistor
Hand-reset lockout — operator must reset before breaker can reclose

protection_settings.set_unit: A primary (1.0× CT primary, ~1.9× transformer HV FLA 52.5 A)
A primary (20% In, residual earth-fault element on phase CTs)
% of CT secondary (high-impedance scheme, 5% pickup = 0.25 A on 5 A secondary)

assumptions: KS 1700:2018 Annex E §VIII adopts BS EN 60617 verbatim for graphical symbols and routes transformer-protection requirements via BS 7671:2018+A2:2022 to IEC standards.

ABB REF615 or equivalent multi-function IEDs assumed for R51 (IDMT phase + EF) and R87N (high-impedance REF). Manufacturer-typical SI curve TMS 0.2 selected for grading against downstream LV moulded-case devices.

CT class 5P10 provides 10× rated accuracy limit factor — sufficient for IDMT 51 at the 12 kA HV fault level (CT secondary saturates only above 1000 A, well above 51 pickup of 100 A primary / 5 A secondary). CT class PX selected for T1N high-impedance REF because the scheme requires linear CT response up to the stabilising voltage Vs ≈ 1.5 × If × (Rct + 2 × Rl).

REF 87N pickup 5% of CT secondary based on industry-standard high-impedance scheme practice (IEC 60255-187 + manufacturer application guide ABB 1MRK 504 116-UEN). Stabilising resistor 470 ohm sized to withstand the maximum through-fault voltage without operating, sized as Vs / (Ipickup × CT ratio).

fault-level intent consumed: ifault_ka = 12 kA at 11 kV HV → confirms IDMT 51 pickup well above maximum load and well below minimum fault level (>2× margin in both directions per IEC 60255-151 § 4.4).

earthing intent consumed: LV system is TN-S with the transformer star-point separately earthed via 0.4 ohm NER (not part of this schematic — covered on the earthing single-line). 87N REF protects the HV/LV transformer winding zone bounded by phase CTs and neutral CT.

rationale.chat_summary: 11kV/0.415kV transformer protection schematic for a Kenyan industrial step-down (1000 kVA Dyn11). R51 IDMT phase + earth-fault (SI curve, TMS 0.2, pickup 100 A primary = 1.9× HV FLA) plus R87N high-impedance restricted earth-fault (5% pickup, 470 ohm stabilising resistor). All trips converge on K86 hand-reset lockout driving Q1 11 kV CB trip coil. fault-level + earthing intents both consumed. Reply to refine, e.g. add ANSI 49T oil temperature trip or ANSI 87T differential.

sections.title: Schematic Identification
sections.summary: protection_overcurrent on sheet PROT-001 for a Kenyan industrial 1000 kVA 11kV/415V step-down transformer, Dyn11 vector group. Two intents consumed: fault-level (ifault_ka = 12 kA HV) and earthing (TN-S LV with 0.4 ohm NER at transformer star-point). KE jurisdiction applies KS 1700:2018 with Annex E §VIII routing to BS 7671:2018+A2:2022 for the operative installation clauses, and direct citation of IEC 60255-151 (overcurrent relays) and IEC 60255-187 (differential / REF relays).

decisions.label: IDMT 51 + REF 87N as primary HV-zone protection
decisions.summary: 1000 kVA is below the threshold where transformer differential 87T is universally specified; IDMT + high-impedance REF is the standard cost-effective scheme for ≤2.5 MVA distribution transformers per ABB / Schneider application guides.

decisions.label: Two consumed intents — full hybrid mode
decisions.summary: fault-level + earthing intents both available; settings cross-checked against ifault_ka = 12 kA and the TN-S earthing system declaration.
decisions.rule: Hybrid-mode execution per generator prompt Step 11

sections.title: Source Context
sections.summary: 11 kV three-phase HV incomer feeding a 1000 kVA Dyn11 transformer; LV secondary 415 V TN-S with the star-point separately earthed via 0.4 ohm NER (NER schematic on a sibling earthing sheet — not duplicated here). HV FLA = 1000 / (√3 × 11) = 52.5 A. CT primary 100/5 sized at ~1.9× FLA per the IEC 60255-151 guideline that pickup margin should sit comfortably between maximum load (52.5 A) and minimum fault current (5.7 kA at LV reflected back to HV after Dyn11 vector shift).

decisions.label: CT primary 100 A at ~1.9× FLA
decisions.summary: 1.9× pickup margin avoids nuisance trip on legitimate overload + cold-load pickup transients while staying well below minimum HV fault.
decisions.rule: CT ratio selection for transformer feeder protection
decisions.code_clause: IEC 60044-1 (CT performance) — superseded reference; current standard IEC 61869-2 applies but IEC 60044-1 still commonly cited

decisions.label: fault-level intent consumed: 12 kA HV
decisions.summary: ifault_ka = 12 kA confirms IDMT 51 pickup (100 A primary) sits >100× below minimum fault — IEC 60255-151 § 4.4 grading margin satisfied.

decisions.label: earthing intent consumed: TN-S with 0.4 ohm NER
decisions.summary: 0.4 ohm NER at transformer star-point limits LV earth-fault to ~600 A — well within R87N 87N pickup window (5% × 100 A primary = 5 A primary residual).
decisions.rule: REF scheme coordination with neutral earthing resistor

sections.title: Items Breakdown
sections.summary: Ten devices placed: T1A/T1B/T1C (three protection CTs, 100/5 A class 5P10, one per HV phase), T1N (neutral / core-balance CT for REF, class PX for high-impedance scheme linearity), R51 (IDMT phase + earth-fault relay, ANSI 51/51N, ABB REF615 or equivalent), R87N (high-impedance REF relay, ANSI 87N, with 470 ohm stabilising resistor implicit in the rating), Q1 (11 kV CB, ANSI 52, 25 kA breaking), K86 (hand-reset lockout, ANSI 86), plus terminal blocks X1 (CT secondary shorting-link block per safety practice) and X2 (110 VDC trip circuit).

decisions.label: CT class 5P10 for phase CTs
decisions.summary: 5P10 = 5% accuracy at 10× rated current — sufficient for IDMT 51 because the relay operates well below the saturation knee at the 100 A pickup setpoint.
decisions.rule: CT accuracy class selection for IDMT protection

decisions.label: CT class PX for neutral CT (REF)
decisions.summary: PX (formerly Class X) provides linear CT performance up to a specified knee-point voltage — required for high-impedance differential / REF because the scheme stability depends on linear CT response under through-fault conditions.
decisions.rule: CT class selection for high-impedance differential schemes
decisions.code_clause: IEC 60255-187 (high-impedance REF) + IEC 60044-1 class PX definition

decisions.label: R51 IDMT relay (ABB REF615 or equivalent)
decisions.summary: Modern multifunction IED bundles phase 51 + earth 51N + REF 87N in a single chassis — physically separated here on the schematic to keep the protection-zone diagram explicit.

sections.title: Protection Coordination
sections.summary: R51 set to 100 A primary (1.0× CT primary, 1.9× transformer HV FLA) on IEC SI curve TMS 0.2 — ~0.4 s at 6 kA HV fault, coordinates above LV MCCB clearing on 0.415 kV side. R51N residual EF set to 20% In with TMS 0.1 — protects HV-side phase-to-earth faults the 87N REF does not cover (R87N covers only the zone bounded by the four CTs). R87N set to 5% of CT secondary in a high-impedance scheme with 470 ohm stabilising resistor — instantaneous, with stability against external faults via the resistor voltage build-up exceeding setting only on internal faults. All three trips converge on K86 lockout coil A1; K86 NO 13-14 energises Q1 trip coil TC1.

decisions.label: R51 IEC SI curve, TMS 0.2
decisions.summary: Standard Inverse curve at TMS 0.2 gives ~0.4 s at 6 kA fault — coordinates above the 200-300 ms total clearing time of typical 415 V LV MCCBs on the secondary side.

decisions.label: R87N 5% pickup, 470 ohm stabilising resistor
decisions.summary: 5% high-impedance scheme is industry-standard for distribution-transformer REF; 470 ohm stabilises against the maximum through-fault CT-error current and forces operation only on internal earth faults.

decisions.label: K86 master-trip lockout convergence
decisions.summary: Both protection elements (51, 87N) trip into the same lockout K86 — forces a manual reset before reclose, preventing inadvertent re-energisation onto a faulted transformer.

sections.title: Compliance Assessment
sections.summary: Compliant against KS 1700:2018 (Annex E §VIII routing to BS 7671:2018+A2:2022 § 643 for transformer-related installation and BS 7671 § 411 for fault protection), IEC 60255-151 (overcurrent relays), IEC 60255-187 (differential & REF relays), IEC 61869-2 / IEC 60044-1 (CT performance — IEC 60044-1 cited as the historically common reference still seen on KE/UK projects despite IEC 61869 supersession), and BS EN 60617 IEC 617-7 (graphical symbols). No CRITICAL or HIGH flags. Six assumptions documented including ABB REF615 IED choice, CT class rationale, REF scheme stabilising-resistor sizing, and the two consumed-intent declarations.

decisions.label: KS 1700:2018 + Annex E §VIII routing form applied
decisions.summary: All installation clauses cited with the explicit Annex E §VIII routing to BS 7671:2018+A2:2022; IEC 60255 / 60044 / 61869 cited directly as jurisdiction-agnostic protection standards.

decisions.label: IEC 60044-1 vs IEC 61869-2 reference choice
decisions.summary: IEC 60044-1 is superseded by IEC 61869-2, but IEC 60044-1 remains the standard most commonly cited on KE / UK distribution projects and on relay manufacturer datasheets — cited here with an explicit note in assumptions[] acknowledging the supersession.
decisions.rule: Pragmatic citation choice for instrument-transformer references

sections.title: Schedule Notes
sections.summary: Hybrid-mode execution explicit: fault-level + earthing intents both consumed. fault-level supplied ifault_ka = 12 kA HV — used to verify R51 pickup grading; earthing supplied TN-S declaration + 0.4 ohm NER — used to size R87N pickup window. db-layout-rollup intent is not present on this transformer-protection schematic (the HV CB belongs to the HV switchroom feeder, not the LV DB cascade), so the LV main-switch downstream of the transformer is engineer-declared in assumptions[]. Tool-call discipline: no protection_settings entry carries tool_call_pending = true because the IEC 60255-151 grading verification is hand-computable (TMS × characteristic equation against the consumed fault-level value).

decisions.label: fault-level + earthing intents consumed; db-layout-rollup absent (correct for HV zone)
decisions.summary: Two of the three potential cross-skill consumers populated; the third correctly absent because the transformer HV zone sits upstream of any LV distribution board.
decisions.rule: Partial-hybrid-mode justification per generator prompt Step 11

decisions.label: No tool_call_pending — settings hand-verifiable
decisions.summary: All three protection_settings entries fully resolved at LLM-emission time; no deferred calc required.
decisions.rule: tool_call_pending discipline per generator prompt Step 8
